turns.prompt: The worst drought in a century. Reservoirs fall to critical levels. Farmers watch crops wither.

choices.label: Introduce strict water rationing
choices.reaction: Meters and penalties appear. Gardens go brown.

choices.label: Fast-track desalination plants

delayed.headline: Desalination plants come online — at a price

choices.reaction: Construction cranes line the coast. Bills will rise.

choices.label: Fine heavy industrial water users
choices.reaction: Factories cut shifts. Smallholders ask why they were first cut off.

turns.prompt: Rain finally comes — but not enough. Groundwater keeps falling. Urban taps sputter.

choices.label: Emergency investment in leaky pipe networks
choices.flavour: Stop losing what you still have.
choices.reaction: Roads open up for repairs. Waste drops — slowly.

choices.label: Import water by tanker and pipeline
choices.reaction: Convoys arrive. Dependence feels uncomfortably real.

choices.label: Prioritise urban supply over agriculture
choices.reaction: Apartment blocks stay supplied. Rural anger simmers.

turns.prompt: Protests block highways. A neighbouring region cuts shared river flows. Diplomacy fails.

choices.label: Offer cash and trade deals for water access
choices.reaction: Flows resume — barely. Neighbours remember the price.

choices.label: Fund controversial cloud-seeding experiments
choices.reaction: Skies darken on schedule — sometimes. Sceptics grow louder.

choices.label: Escort water convoys with security forces
choices.flavour: Treat water like a strategic asset.
choices.reaction: Supplies arrive under guard. The mood turns authoritarian.

turns.prompt: The aquifer collapses faster than models predicted. Every earlier choice left someone thirsty.

memory_callbacks.text: Rural communities you sidelined now block the roads into the capital.
Desalination cannot scale fast enough for the inland cities.
Industrial fines you levied closed plants that supplied the cities.

choices.label: Evacuate the driest regions
choices.flavour: Abandon land that cannot sustain people.
choices.reaction: Relocation camps swell. Ghost towns spread.

choices.label: Declare a water emergency with martial powers
choices.reaction: Queues become orderly. Freedom feels distant.

choices.label: Launch an international humanitarian appeal
choices.flavour: Admit you cannot solve this alone.
choices.reaction: Aid arrives with cameras. National pride bruises.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What water future did you leave?

choices.label: Rebuild around conservation and reuse
choices.flavour: Live within the rain you get.
choices.reaction: Usage falls permanently. Growth slows — deliberately.

choices.label: Bet on mega-engineering to control supply
choices.flavour: Dam, pipe, and pump your way out.
choices.reaction: Infrastructure sprawls. Ecologists warn of new risks.

choices.label: Formalise fair sharing with neighbouring regions
choices.reaction: Rivers flow under rules. Sovereignty feels shared — and the meter reader agrees.
